pi_items.source: setup_task.sh generates exactly 30 participants in the dataset
Calculated directly from the synthetic data generated in setup_task.sh (Sum=2072, N=30). The expected value 68.5 is an arithmetic error.
Calculated directly from the synthetic data generated in setup_task.sh (76 successes out of 90 tasks = 84.44%). The expected value 82.2% is an arithmetic error.
setup_task.sh explicitly writes to this path

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated participant usability data. While the expected sample size of 30, slide count of 5, and output paths are confirmed, I discovered a critical defect in the task. The expected metrics in the verifier (SUS score of 68.5 and success rate of 82.2%) are contradicted by the actual injected data. Accurate calculation of the provided dataset yields a true average SUS score of 69.07 and an overall task success rate of 84.44% (76 out of 90 tasks). Consequently, an agent performing the calculations correctly will strictly fail the verifier's hardcoded success rate check.
